query: BJP Jharkhand Rajya Sabha candidate name June 2026 latest news
answer:
## Research Report: BJP Jharkhand Rajya Sabha Candidate — June 2026

### Key Finding: No Official Candidate Announced Yet

As of June 1, 2026, **BJP has not yet officially announced its Rajya Sabha candidate from Jharkhand**. The filing deadline is **June 8, 2026**, so the announcement is expected imminently. The retiring BJP incumbent whose seat is up is **Deepak Prakash** (term: June 22, 2020 – June 21, 2026).

---

### Election Schedule

The Election Commission of India announced on **May 22, 2026** that biennial elections for **24 Rajya Sabha seats** across 10 states will be held, including **2 seats from Jharkhand**.

| Event | Date |
|---|---|
| Notification | June 1, 2026 |
| Last date for nominations | June 8, 2026 |
| Scrutiny of nominations | June 9, 2026 |
| Last date for withdrawal | June 11, 2026 |
| **Poll & Result** | **June 18, 2026** |

### The Two Jharkhand Seats

1. **Deepak Prakash (BJP)** — retiring incumbent, term expiring June 21, 2026.
2. **Vacant** — following the death of JMM supremo **Shibu Soren** on August 4, 2025 (age 81). His term was also set to expire June 21, 2026.

### Electoral Math: BJP Faces an Uphill Battle

The election quota is **(81 MLAs ÷ 3) + 1 = 28 votes** per seat.

- **Ruling MGB/INDIA Alliance: 56 MLAs** (JMM 34, Congress 16, RJD 4, CPI(ML)(L) 2) — enough for **both seats**.
- **Opposition NDA: 24 MLAs** (BJP 21, AJSU 1, JD(U) 1, LJP(RV) 1) — **4 votes short** of the 28-vote quota.

BJP would need **cross-voting from at least 4 ruling alliance MLAs** to win even one seat.

### Political Context

- **BJP leaders Babulal Marandi** (Leader of Opposition) and **Aditya Sahu** (State President) have publicly confirmed BJP will field a candidate despite the numbers disadvantage.
- **JMM filed a complaint** with the Election Commission on May 25, 2026, alleging BJP is attempting **horse-trading** — trying to lure ruling alliance MLAs through financial inducements and pressure.
- Within the ruling alliance, **Congress** has expressed interest in one of the two seats, with the expected split being one for JMM and one for Congress.
- If alliance discipline holds, Jharkhand's RS composition would shift from BJP 3 / JMM 2 / 1 vacant → **BJP 2 / JMM-INDIA Alliance 4**.

---

key_facts: BJP has NOT officially announced its Rajya Sabha candidate from Jharkhand as of June 1, 2026; the filing deadline is June 8, 2026.
The retiring BJP incumbent is Deepak Prakash, whose term expires June 21, 2026.
Two Rajya Sabha seats from Jharkhand are up for election on June 18, 2026 — one held by BJP (Deepak Prakash) and one vacant after Shibu Soren's death on August 4, 2025.
BJP leaders Babulal Marandi (Leader of Opposition) and Aditya Sahu (State President) have confirmed BJP will contest despite unfavorable numbers.
The ruling MGB/INDIA Alliance holds 56 of 81 MLAs — enough to win both seats (quota: 28 each).
BJP-led NDA has only 24 MLAs — 4 short of the 28-vote quota needed for one seat.
JMM filed a horse-trading complaint with the Election Commission on May 25, 2026, alleging BJP is trying to lure ruling alliance MLAs.
Congress has expressed interest in contesting one of the two Jharkhand seats within the ruling alliance.
